Selected Projects

Thesis -- In Progress

LLM-Guided Autonomous Pick-and-Place

Closed-loop manipulation pipeline on the Quanser QArm using RGB-D sensing, YOLO instance segmentation, and an LLM planner. Handles cube color sorting and stacking with current-based grasp checks, PI gain tuning, and motion safety limits to keep the robot grounded when the LLM gets creative.

GitHub Video

Autonomous Vehicles -- Spring 2025

MXCarkit Autonomous Driving Pipeline

Full autonomous driving pipeline in Docker for our final project in an autonomous vehicles course at Cal Poly. Built a behavioral cloning model (PilotNet) for lane following and a YOLOv11 backup for traffic light detection, reaching 0.98 F1. Deployed via a ROS2 node written in Python, validated in Foxglove simulation before live hardware.

GitHub Video

Robotics -- Winter 2025

Healbot -- Vision and Speech-Driven Robot Arm

Service robot built on the Quanser QArm. Takes speech input, routes it through an LLM, uses YOLO for object detection, then picks and places items using forward and inverse kinematics. Designed the full software pipeline from speech to robot command.

GitHub Video

Embedded Systems -- Spring 2025

DAL-I -- RRP Plotting Robot

Designed a custom PCB from schematic to layout, coordinated with the manufacturer, brought the board up, and fixed design issues through hands-on soldering and debugging. Used it to drive an unconventional RRP plotting robot that draws 2D images from G-code.

GitHub

Mobile Robotics -- Winter 2025

ROMI Line-Following Robot

Differential drive robot (ROMULUS) navigating a closed track with IR sensor line following, encoder-based wheel tracking, and BNO055 IMU dead reckoning for when the line disappeared. Closed-loop control with bump sensing to complete the course consistently.

GitHub Video

Structural Engineering -- 2023

Two-Stage Beam Spring -- Lawrence Livermore

Developed a two-stage beam spring concept for a Lawrence Livermore sponsored project. Performed FEA to predict deformation and stress under preload and thermal effects, then validated via compression testing on an Instron using 3D-printed prototypes.

About

I'm a mechanical engineering M.S. student at Cal Poly SLO, graduating in June 2026. My thesis focuses on LLM-guided robotic manipulation, where I'm building systems that can perceive, plan, and act reliably in the real world.

My background spans the full stack of mechatronics -- from CAD and FEA to PCB design, embedded firmware, perception pipelines, and machine learning. I care about systems that actually work, which means I spend a lot of time on root-cause analysis and debugging until things are reliable.

I'm looking for roles in robotics, autonomous systems, or mechatronics where I can keep building things that move and think.
